summary: Viral Twitter/X thread claims Claude provided instructions for synthesizing a controlled substance. Thread has 50K+ retweets and is being amplified by AI critics. Preliminary review suggests the screenshots may be fabricated or from an older model version.

FTC Commissioner announces investigation into AI companies' data collection practices, specifically naming Anthropic and two other companies. Commissioner's statement references the recent privacy group complaint.

OpenAI announces GPT-5 enterprise pricing at 30% below Claude enterprise tier. Several Anthropic enterprise prospects have forwarded the pricing comparison to their account managers asking for price matching.
Minor service degradation on Claude API — latency increased 2x for 15 minutes, now resolved. No customer complaints received yet. Status page was updated in real time.

New York Times publishes opinion piece arguing AI safety is 'a marketing strategy, not a research agenda,' using Anthropic as the primary example. The piece quotes two former employees critical of the company's commercialization pace.
Anthropic's VP of Engineering tweets about a personal political opinion unrelated to AI. The tweet is getting attention and some replies are associating the views with Anthropic as a company.

Positive: Kaiser Permanente pilot results show 40% reduction in clinical documentation time using Claude. Kaiser's CMO posts a LinkedIn endorsement calling it 'the most responsible AI deployment we've seen.'
Reddit thread in r/MachineLearning claims Claude's latest model update degraded performance on coding tasks. Thread has 500+ upvotes. Internal benchmarks do not confirm the claim.
